OZo Ads

A transparent, end-to-end platform that lets any business — local café to global brand — discover, book, and manage outdoor advertising campaigns without agencies, guesswork, or hidden costs.

Role: UI / UX Designer

Contribution: Website Design, Visual Design, Prototyping

·
Final Result
View Figma File
Design Process
UX Case Study · 2025–26

Billboard ads,
without
the chaos.

A transparent, end-to-end platform that lets any business — local café to global brand — discover, book, and manage outdoor advertising campaigns without agencies, guesswork, or hidden costs.

UX ResearchProduct Strategy
4×
Persona types
7+
Pain clusters
12
Edge cases mapped
6
Journey stages
Local → Global
Scales from Tier-2 cities to 50+ countries
🌍
01 — The Problem

Billboard advertising is broken
by design.

What should be a powerful offline channel is trapped behind phone calls, opaque pricing, fragmented vendors, and zero measurement.

"I just want to book it online without calling multiple people."

Billboard advertising remains one of the most effective forms of brand awareness — but for most businesses, it's completely inaccessible without an agency or inside connections. Pricing is hidden. Availability is a phone call. Proof of display is a WhatsApp photo.

The gap between the potential of OOH advertising and the reality of booking it is enormous — and it disproportionately hurts small businesses and new market entrants who need it most.

Discovery overload
Don't know where to start, no audience data, no inventory visibility
Pricing opacity
Hidden costs, no benchmarks, fear of overpaying, multi-currency confusion
Low trust & ROI
No proof of display, no verification, no performance metrics
Operational friction
Manual emails & calls, multiple vendors, no central dashboard
Creative chaos
Unknown specs, rejection risk, version control nightmares
Rigidity
Monthly-only bookings, no pausing, no experimentation
Emotional gap
Post-payment anxiety, no status updates, no human support when stuck
02 — Who We're Designing For

Four users, one platform.

From a local café owner to a global marketing director — the platform must serve radically different needs without sacrificing depth or simplicity.

M
Maria Fernandes
Local Café Owner · Mid-sized city
Budget: Low1st-timerTrust-driven
I don't need fancy marketing — I just want to know it'll actually bring people in.
A
Arjun Mehta
D2C Startup Founder · Metro city
Budget: MidData-drivenFast-moving
If I can't test, tweak, and measure it — it's not worth my budget.
S
Sarah Thompson
National Marketing Manager
Budget: HighProcess-firstReporting-heavy
I don't want surprises — I want visibility, control, and clean reports.
D
Daniel Wong
Global Marketing Director · Singapore/London
Budget: Very highRisk-averseGovernance
If I can't see it, verify it, and govern it — it's a liability.
03 — Empathy Map

Inside the user's head.

Synthesizing across all four personas to understand the shared emotional and behavioral landscape.

💬Says
"I want to advertise in a busy area, but I don't know where to start.
"Why is billboard pricing so unclear?
"I just want to book it online without calling multiple people.
"I need something quick and reliable.
⚙️Does
"Searches online for billboard advertising options
"Compares locations, prices, and visibility
"Uploads creatives or checks design requirements
"Reviews confirmation, payment, and campaign status
💭Thinks
"Am I paying too much for this location?
"Will this billboard actually get enough visibility?
"I hope this platform is trustworthy.
"I need proof this is worth my money.
❤️Feels
"Confused at the start — too many unknowns
"Anxious about cost and campaign effectiveness
"Cautiously optimistic when seeing clear options
"Relieved and confident once tracking is live
04 — User Journey Map

From “how do I start?”
to “my ad is live.”

Six stages. Every friction point, feeling, and opportunity catalogued.

#StageUser ActionsEmotionsOpportunity
01
Discover
Awareness
Visits platform, searches for billboard options, enters preferred city, views mapCuriousUnsureHopeful
→Clear "Find Billboards Near You" entry point
→Location-based recommendations
→Simple hero messaging, no jargon
02
Evaluate
Research
Clicks billboard pins, views photos & specs, compares pricing, checks estimated reachInterestedConfusedCautious
→Side-by-side comparison view
→Real-world photo previews with street context
→Plain-language traffic & audience data
03
Select & Schedule
Decision
Selects billboard, chooses dates, reviews availability calendar, confirms durationFocusedAnxiousHopeful
→Price lock timer (10 min hold)
→Availability warnings & scarcity signals
→Clear cost summary before next step
04
Upload Creative
Production
Views requirements, uploads file, previews on billboard mockup, fixes errorsUncertainRelievedConfident
→Free templates for common billboard sizes
→Real-time mockup preview on actual billboard photo
→Live file validation (format, size, resolution)
05
Review & Pay
Commitment
Reviews campaign summary, checks total cost, enters payment, completes bookingNervousTrustingRelieved
→Clear refund & cancellation policies upfront
→Secure payment indicators (SSL, trust badges)
→Itemised cost breakdown before confirming
06
Track & Measure
Post-booking
Receives confirmation, views campaign dashboard, tracks status, reviews post-campaign summarySatisfiedConfidentAccomplished
→Real-time status notifications (SMS + email)
→Proof-of-display photos once live
→Simple performance summary + easy rebooking
05 — How Might We

Reframing problems
as opportunities.

Using the HMW framework to unlock design directions for each persona's most critical challenges.

Maria — Local Business Owner
Remove the bad
Help small businesses advertise without fearing wasted spend?
Change the status quo
Make booking a billboard as simple as boosting a social media post?
Question an assumption
Let small businesses try billboard advertising without long-term commitment or large upfront costs?
Arjun — Startup Founder
Change the status quo
Allow startups to test billboard campaigns as easily as running A/B tests online?
Amp up the good
Turn offline billboard data into actionable growth insights for founders?
Remove the bad
Eliminate long approval cycles that slow down startup marketing experiments?
Sarah — National Marketing Manager
Break into pieces
Centralize planning, booking, and reporting for multi-city billboard campaigns?
Change the status quo
Make OOH planning as measurable and optimizable as digital media?
Identify unexpected resources
Use real-time performance signals to optimize placements mid-campaign?
Daniel — Global Brand Director
Change the status quo
Manage global billboard campaigns from a single unified platform?
Remove the bad
Reduce compliance risks across different countries and advertising regulations?
Create an analogy
Make global OOH management feel like one dashboard — not 20 local ones?
06 — Problem Statements & Hypotheses

If we solve this,
here's what changes.

Grounding each persona's problem in a testable hypothesis that drives design decisions.

Maria — Local Business Owner
If / Then

If Maria is guided through a step-by-step booking flow with clear pricing and visual previews, then she will feel confident enough to complete her first billboard booking.

We believe

Simplifying the booking process and removing pricing ambiguity will increase billboard adoption among local small businesses.

Arjun — Startup Founder
If / Then

If Arjun can compare billboard locations, pricing, and estimated reach in one place, then he will make faster and more confident campaign decisions.

We believe

Providing comparison tools and basic performance metrics will help startup founders scale billboard advertising more effectively.

Sarah — National Marketing Manager
If / Then

If Sarah can book, edit, and report on multiple billboards from one dashboard, then she can execute national campaigns more efficiently and accurately.

We believe

Bulk booking, approvals, and standardized reporting will reduce operational overhead for national marketing teams.

Daniel — Global Marketing Director
If / Then

If Daniel can manage global campaigns with localized pricing, compliance guidance, and time-zone-aware scheduling, then he can launch international campaigns faster and with less risk.

We believe

Centralizing global billboard operations will improve speed, consistency, and confidence for international brands.

07 — Value Proposition

Features become benefits
when designed with intent.

Every feature exists to close a specific gap between what users fear and what they need to feel confident.

"

A single, transparent platform that lets any business discover, book, and manage billboard advertising worldwide — without vendors, guesswork, or hidden costs.

🗺️
Discovery replaces guesswork
Map-based search with real photos, traffic data, and audience demographics — users understand billboard value before spending anything.
💰
Pricing is never a surprise
Transparent cost breakdowns, price locks, and comparison tools mean users feel financially safe — even on small budgets.
✅
Creative confidence before go-live
Templates, real-time validation, and billboard mockup previews eliminate rejection anxiety and spec confusion.
📊
Proof turns payment into trust
Verified vendors, go-live notifications, and proof-of-display photos give users accountability through the full campaign lifecycle.
08 — Feature Architecture

Everything the platform
needs to deliver.

Grouped by the user value they unlock — not by technical category.

🗺️Discovery
  • ·Map-based billboard discovery
  • ·Location & city search
  • ·Filters: price, traffic, type, size
  • ·Real billboard photos + street view
  • ·Audience & traffic indicators
  • ·Save to wishlist
  • ·Side-by-side comparison
💳Pricing & Payments
  • ·Upfront, transparent pricing
  • ·Itemised cost breakdown
  • ·Price lock timer
  • ·Multi-currency support
  • ·Secure checkout
  • ·Invoices, receipts & tax docs
  • ·Refund & cancellation policies
📅Booking & Scheduling
  • ·Step-by-step booking wizard
  • ·Availability calendar (real-time)
  • ·Flexible durations (1 week–custom)
  • ·Time-zone-aware scheduling
  • ·Auto-saved drafts
  • ·Confirmation + go-live alerts
🎨Creative Management
  • ·Creative upload with validation
  • ·Billboard mockup live preview
  • ·Free design templates
  • ·Version control
  • ·Brand asset library
  • ·Format & resolution checker
📊Analytics & Reports
  • ·Estimated impressions & reach
  • ·Campaign delivery progress
  • ·Proof-of-display photos
  • ·Post-campaign summaries
  • ·Downloadable reports (PDF/CSV)
  • ·Historical campaign archive
🏢Enterprise & Teams
  • ·Role-based access control
  • ·Approval workflows
  • ·Bulk booking & editing
  • ·Audit logs
  • ·Multi-user collaboration
  • ·Conflict detection
🌍Global & Compliance
  • ·Multi-language UI
  • ·Local ad regulation guidance
  • ·Tax & compliance notes
  • ·Country-specific formatting
  • ·Currency normalization
🛡️Trust & Support
  • ·Verified vendor badges
  • ·Live chat & ticket support
  • ·Help center & onboarding
  • ·FAQs & guided tooltips
  • ·Auto-save & offline resilience
09 — Edge Cases & Safeguards

Designing for
when things go wrong.

Booking systems fail in predictable ways. Every edge case has a designed response.

EC — 01
Billboard taken mid-booking
Another advertiser books while user is in checkout flow.
Temporary hold + countdown timer
EC — 02
Price changes after selection
Demand or seasonality updates the price after user selects dates.
Price lock for 10 minutes
EC — 03
Creative fails compliance
Uploaded ad violates local advertising regulations or size specs.
Clear error + guided fix suggestions
EC — 04
Payment succeeds, booking fails
System or vendor error after successful payment.
Auto-refund + confirmation email
EC — 05
Vendor cancels post-confirmation
Billboard owner cancels due to maintenance or regulatory issues.
Alternatives surfaced + platform credits
EC — 06
Time zone scheduling error
Global brand schedules without realizing local time zone difference.
Time-zone-aware preview at scheduling step
EC — 07
Wrong location selected
User misreads map and books a similar nearby billboard by mistake.
Confirmation step with map + 24hr free cancel
EC — 08
Internet lost mid-booking
Local business owner loses connectivity while completing checkout.
Auto-save drafts at every step
EC — 09
Simultaneous team edits
Two users make conflicting changes to the same campaign at once.
Conflict detection + version locking
10 — User Stories

Designing from
real user needs.

Eight stories across four personas — each one a design requirement in disguise.

As a local small business owner
I want to see nearby billboards with traffic and visibility indicators
So that I can confidently choose a location without feeling overwhelmed or misled.
As a budget-conscious café owner
I want to see clear, upfront pricing before I commit
So that I don't fear hidden costs or wasting money.
As a startup founder running experiments
I want to book short-term campaigns and adjust or pause them easily
So that I can test ideas quickly without long-term risk.
As a growth marketer at a startup
I want to compare locations by cost, reach, and audience type
So that I can allocate my limited budget to the highest-impact options.
As a startup founder
I want to upload and preview my creative on real billboard mockups
So that I can be confident my brand looks professional in the real world.
As a national brand marketing manager
I want a centralized dashboard to manage bookings, creatives, and approvals across regions
So that I can maintain brand consistency and reduce operational chaos.
As a marketing manager
I want proof-of-display and campaign timelines for each billboard
So that I can confidently report execution accuracy to leadership.
As a global marketing director
I want standardized metrics, compliance checks, and currency-normalized pricing across countries
So that I can scale campaigns globally without legal or financial risk.

OZo Ads · TinyPixel Studio
